curation_notes: Extractor drafted a MONETARY POLICY bundle, not an independence-conflict bundle. Dropped the whole market-mechanics pillar (monetary policy, interest rate, rate hike/cut/decision, forward guidance, inflation, price stability, balance sheet, stress test, inflation hawk, policy meeting, quiet period). Aliases are OR'd, so on a bare-country gate any one of those admits every US economics story.

MEASURED AND DROPPED THE BARE INSTITUTION. 'Fed' takes the case-sensitive acronym path and matched 1229 titles, but a read of the sample put it at only ~50% on-topic for an INDEPENDENCE atomic -- the rest is Treasuries, gold, rate bets, Fed minutes, fintech payment accounts, NY Fed FX checks. That is well below the A2 bar (Greenland ~95%), i.e. the Australia lesson: A2 is available only when the subject's coverage IS the dispute, and Fed coverage is half ordinary central banking. Dropped bare Fed / Federal Reserve / central bank and anchored on the officers and the removal-and-appointment fight instead. Measured result: 585 titles / 85 non-EN / 82 publishers -- sharper AND larger than the 470 pre-estimate.

Same logic applied to the non-English bare forms, which take the SUBSTRING path and would each re-admit all Fed coverage in that language: dropped bare ФРС, 美联储, FRB, الاحتياطي الفيدرالي. Kept only their compound chair/governor/independence forms.

Dropped 'Supreme Court': belongs to us_judicial_constraint, and bare 'Supreme Court' is a 1798-title firehose (India/Korea/Nigeria/Estonia/Australia). The Lisa Cook ruling still lands here via 'Lisa Cook' + 'Fed governor'.
Dropped generic process nouns firing/sack/subpoena/criminal probe/Senate confirmation/Board of Governors -- OR'd generics on a country gate.
Dropped 'John Williams' (NY Fed president) -- collides with the composer, who is far more frequent.
Dropped bare 'Bowman' (generic surname); full name only.
NEVER add bare 'Warsh': the EN matcher is word-START prefix, so \mWarsh matches 'warship'/'warships' -- 698 raw vs ~150 real.

'Powell' pre-audited at 256 titles and is 100% Jerome Powell, and the content is the dispute itself (DOJ probe into Powell, Trump threatening to fire him, the independence quote, Warsh replacing him). Kept.
Added 'Fed chief' and de 'Fed-Vorständin'/'Fed-Vorstand': the order rule makes phrases literal, and these forms appear in the corpus (FAZ: 'Gericht lässt Fed-Vorständin im Amt').
